_meta.note: Astryx canon corpus — keyword retrieval (Phase 1). Regenerate via `npm run build:canon`.

chunks.text: Astryx is a deterministic multi-sensory calibration system. From birth data it reads a natal chart as a pattern-recognition map and generates a personalized six-sense protocol — Sound, Scent, Taste, Body, Sight, and Field (the user's own auric/etheric field, which the fork's vibration moves through and clears). Same birth data always produces the same output. It is calibration, not prediction; a blueprint, not a reading. It never diagnoses, treats, or prescribes. (Astryx herself is the app's guide — the intelligence that explains the reading — not one of the six senses.)
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: The core flow: Intake (name, birth date, birth time or 'unknown', birth city, symptoms, emotional state, intention) → Analysis (a brief sacred-geometry loader while the engine computes) → Today's Reading (one card naming the signal, carrier, and today's fork sequence) → Resonance Chamber (the session) → Dashboard (the daily home with check-in, today's pulse, summary, explore-deeper, chart, body map, history).
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: How to read the reading: the engine ranks a SIGNAL HIERARCHY — a primary signal (the surface, what you feel), a secondary (the chart root it traces to), and a tertiary (the current aggravator). Each carries a polarity state: excess (running hot), deficiency (running low), blocked (held/stuck), or balanced, with a confidence band. The five-sense protocol and the chamber are chosen to move the signal toward balance — never to amplify it.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: The Planet is not the Remedy. A dominant planet is not automatically amplified. The Remedy Polarity engine first reads whether that planet runs excess, deficient, or blocked, then chooses a corrective direction — often borrowing a regulator planet's qualities — so the protocol cools an excess or warms a deficiency. This 'never amplify' rule runs through the whole system, including the chamber forks.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: The Resonance Chamber is a full guided session: a fullscreen visual field, layered planetary tuning-fork music, and a guided breath. A phase ribbon shows progress; the fork sequence advances phase by phase. Four visual modes — Body, Color, Mandala, Combined — are switched from a toggle at the top, and the choice never restarts the audio or the timer.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: How the chamber chooses your forks (corrective sessions): a composition engine lays down several DISTINCT forks, one per phase, drawn from your signal hierarchy (primary, secondary, tertiary), your stated intention, and any planets you marked as already steady (resources). If a signal runs hot (excess or blocked), the chamber leads with its calming counterweight rather than repeating the loud planet. 15-minute sessions use 4 distinct forks, 30-minute use 6, 60-minute use 8.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: Earth bookends: every chamber session opens with an Earth Day tone under the first phase and closes with an Earth Year tone under the last — a grounding open and a grounding close. These are audio only; the on-screen planet and visuals belong to the phase itself.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: Full-Spectrum Recalibration is a fourth session type: a whole-body attunement (not a correction). It opens with breathwork (Earth Day underneath), then sounds all ten planetary forks from the feet upward — Neptune at the feet, then Uranus, Saturn, Jupiter, Pluto, Venus, Mercury, Sun, Moon, and Mars at the head — each at its natural, balanced tone, then closes with breathwork (Earth Year underneath). It does not target today's signal; it tones the whole field evenly.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: The six-sense protocol: Sound (planetary tones from Hans Cousto's cosmic octave), Scent (essential oils mapped to the active planet), Taste (an herbal blend or tea), Body (a breath, posture, and orientation), Sight (a color and a sacred-geometry form tied to the dominant aspect), and Field (the user's own auric/etheric field — the space around the body that the fork's vibration moves through and clears; the aluminum field forks especially, sounded off-body). The five sensory channels are chosen by the corrective polarity so the protocol moves toward balance; Field is the universal effect of the sound spreading through the surrounding field.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: The Body Map shows where to hold each fork. Astryx renders two glowing orbs per fork: a TRADITIONAL placement (the planet's rulership home — the classical zodiacal-man body region) and a NATAL placement (where that planet actually sits in your chart, by its natal sign). When a planet is in its own ruling sign the two merge into one orb. Pelvic and reproductive placements always sit on the womb/sacral field — never the genitals — and intimate placements use an off-body sweep with no contact.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: Body-Map play-tones: tapping a chakra plays its Solfeggio tone; tapping a planet's body region plays that planet's Cousto fork tone. One plays at a time; tapping another switches; closing the panel stops it.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: Solar Chart mode: when you don't know your birth time, Astryx places the Sun on the Ascendant and aligns the houses to sign boundaries. All ten planetary positions and aspects stay astronomically accurate; only house-based detail is approximate. It is always clearly labelled '☉ Solar Chart'. You can switch back any time by entering a birth time.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: The chamber music has a Default and a Customize chooser. Default lets Astryx pick the song for each fork's planet and state deterministically. Customize lets you pick a specific variant per planet+state; your choice is saved and persists across sessions, and a repeated fork in one session advances to a different variant rather than replaying the same track. '↺ Reset all' clears your custom choices.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: Three access tiers. Individual ($9.95/mo): plain-language diagnostics, prescriptions, transit weather, the daily home, and a metered daily allowance of Ask Astryx questions. Practitioner ($39.95/mo, self-attested): clinical terminology, classical citations, a client roster, session notes, PDF export, and unmetered Ask Astryx. Verified Practitioner ($59/mo): a verified badge, credential auto-fill, and insurance-grade SOAP. A legacy XRP payment (5 XRP) unlocks a year of equivalent access.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: Transit weather (Cosmic Weather): Astryx recomputes the live sky against your natal chart each day, surfacing today's temperature (cool/warm/hot), the headline transit, and the active transits — so the daily reading reflects what the sky is doing now, not just your birth chart.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: Ask Astryx is the app's guide — the living intelligence of the system. She explains your existing reading, defines terms, connects the frameworks, and teaches you to read your own chart over time. She is the guide, not one of the senses (the sixth sense is your own auric Field). She never changes your reading, never diagnoses, and always speaks in probabilistic language. Her goal is fluency — a user who practices should need her less over time.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: Sacred Tones are real physical tuning forks tuned to the Cousto cosmic-octave frequencies. The chamber's on-screen tones mirror them; with the physical forks a practitioner applies the named fork to the mapped body point for a client's chart. The fork-to-body-point intelligence is the Practitioner Portal's crown feature.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: The Full Body Recalibration is one of the chamber's three session modes — the complete anatomical ladder, all twelve forks, ground to crown and back. It opens with an Earth ground, climbs the zodiacal body feet to head, pauses for three slow breaths at the crown turn, then sweeps back down the same ladder to an Earth ground close. Every rung sounds its planet's natural tone — an attunement of the whole map, not a correction of one signal. It is the same sequence for every body and needs no reading.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: The Full Body ladder order, feet to head: Pisces (feet · Neptune), Aquarius (shins · Uranus), Capricorn (knees · Saturn), Sagittarius (hips · Jupiter), Scorpio (pelvis region · Pluto), Libra (lower back · Venus), Virgo (intestines region · Mercury), Leo (solar plexus region · Sun), Cancer (chest region · Moon), Gemini (shoulders and hands · Mercury), Taurus (thymus and throat region · Venus), Aries (head · Mars). The descent retraces the same rungs as a lighter sweep. Mercury and Venus each strike twice — two distinct placements each.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: Choosing between the session modes: a Calibrated Session is tuned to your chart and today's sky — the composition engine selects the forks your reading calls for, honoring the never-amplify rule. The Full Body Recalibration is the complete twelve-fork anatomical ladder, the same map for everyone. The Chakra Recalibration works the seven centers, root to crown and back, with your choice of Solfeggio or Planetary forks. The whole-body and chakra modes suit full-coverage days or a weekly reset — and need no reading; the Calibrated Session is the targeted daily work. All three live in the Resonance Chamber and close on the Earth ground.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: The Chakra Recalibration is a seven-center chamber session: Opening Ground, then Root, Sacral, Solar Plexus, Heart, Throat, Third Eye, and Crown ascending, three slow breaths at the crown, the same centers swept back down, and an Earth ground close. At the start you choose your instrument set — Solfeggio forks (Root 396 Hz, Sacral 417, Solar Plexus 528, Heart 639, Throat 741, Third Eye 852, Crown 963) or Planetary forks (Root · Earth Day, Sacral · Full Moon, Solar Plexus · Sun, Heart · Venus, Throat · Mercury, Third Eye · Neptune, Crown · Jupiter). The chamber carries any tone you do not hold in hand, so no center is ever skipped. Both sets run the same deterministic sequence.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

chunks.text: Astryx can set the chamber up for you. When you ask about today's transits, a session, the Full Body ladder, or the chakras, a button appears under her reply that carries the session directly — today's calibration already tuned by the engine, the Full Body ladder, or the Chakra Recalibration with your instrument set. Tapping it closes the chat and opens the chamber configured and ready. Astryx never computes or changes the session itself — the engine's deterministic output decides every fork; she only opens the door. If today's check-in hasn't happened yet, the button routes through the Daily Check-In first so the calibration is never skipped. The same tiles live on the Dashboard under Sessions.
chunks.source: Astryx product canon (distilled from the user guide, capabilities manifest, and live app behavior)

Biological: PrimaryFunction: Building block of brain and nerve cell tissue; cellular antiseptic that counteracts organic decay; supports neurotransmitter synthesis and gray matter integrity · Mechanism: Concentrated in tissues and fluids of brain and nerve cells. Has antiseptic action that prevents cellular decay. Essential constituent of nerve fluid — the chemical base of material expression and understanding itself per Carey. · BodySystems: central nervous system, cerebrospinal axis, brain · OrgansGoverned: brain, cerebrum, optic thalamus, head, face, scalp · EndocrineTarget: Pineal gland and pituitary body (Carey's 'third eye / optic thalamus' axis) · NervousSystemRole: Primary mineral basis of all nerve tissue. Critical for cortical function, signal transmission, and parasympathetic recovery. Pairs with Mag Phos for nervous system rescue.
Deficiency: Physical: nervous headaches, insomnia (nervous origin), shingles, nervous asthma, nervous dyspepsia, bleeding gums, halitosis, periodontosis, dry tongue · Emotional: irritability, grumpiness, despondency, groundless fears, easily discouraged, languid weariness, absent expression · Mental: mental exhaustion, brain fog, lowered vitality, inability to concentrate, memory weakness, burnout · FacialSigns:…

CellSalt: CommonName: Natrum Sulphuricum · ShortName: Nat Sulph · SaltNumber: 11 · ChemicalName: Sodium Sulphate · ChemicalFormula: Na2SO4 · CareyTitle: Water Eliminator · Epithet: The Liver Salt
Biological: PrimaryFunction: Regulates the density of intercellular fluids by eliminating excess water; governs liver function and bile production; clears auto-intoxication from the system · Mechanism: One molecule of Nat Sulph removes two molecules of water from the body. Excess atmospheric water absorbed through the lungs is eliminated by this salt. Without it, waste fluids accumulate in blood and tissue causing self-poisoning. · BodySystems: hepatobiliary system, lymphatic drainage, intercellular fluid regulation · OrgansGoverned: throat, neck, cerebellum (lower brain), thyroid zone, liver, gallbladder · EndocrineTarget: Thyroid gland, liver-bile axis · NervousSystemRole: Supports cerebellum function (Carey's 'lower brain') — coordination, balance, and the foundation of the spinal column. Liver congestion creates back-of-head tension extending down the spine.
Deficiency: Physical: biliousness, liver congestion, influenza, watery infiltrations, back-of-head pain extending down spine, sandy urine deposits, bitter taste in mouth, throat irritation, neck tightness, rheumatic ailments · Emotional: sluggishness, melancholy, irritability with bile excess, stubborn heaviness · Mental: mental dullness from toxic load, morning grogginess, hepatic depression · Faci…

CellSalt: CommonName: Kali Muriaticum · ShortName: Kali Mur · SaltNumber: 5 · ChemicalName: Potassium Chloride · ChemicalFormula: KCl · CareyTitle: Blood Purifier & Glandular Tonic · Epithet: The Fibrin Builder
Biological: PrimaryFunction: Builds and diffuses fibrin throughout the body; produces saliva and supports early-stage digestion; cleanses blood and lymphatic discharge · Mechanism: Combines with organic fibrin. Deficiency causes fibrin to thicken and discharge as thick white catarrh, mucus, or coatings — the body's signal that fibrin is misfiring. The copper-resonant field interacts with the dense pelvic nerve plexuses, stimulating deep cellular release and smooth muscle relaxation in reproductive tissues. · BiologicalSystem: Reproductive Endocrine & Circulatory · EndocrineTarget: Gonads (ovaries/testes — deep pelvic plexus), liver (copper-dependent enzyme support) · NervousSystem: Deep pelvic plexus (inferior hypogastric plexus) activation; sacral parasympathetic stimulation; smooth muscle relaxation in reproductive cavity · BodyPlacement: Pelvic bowl (2-3 inches below navel, over reproductive organs) · PlacementNote: Place polished Malachite 2-3 inches below the navel over the pelvic cavity while lying flat. Hold 15-20 minutes. ALWAYS use polished and sealed Malachite only. · SafetyNote: CRITICAL — Only polished/sealed Malachite on skin. Raw Malachite contains toxic copper carbonate dust that must NOT contact moisture or bare skin. Never use raw Malachite in a layout. Do not use internally or in water. · KitEligible: true · SafetyLevel: Handle with care

Variety: Red Lotus
LatinName: Nelumbo nucifera (red variety)
SanskritName: Kamala
PrimaryPlanet: Mars
SecondaryPlanet: Sun
Element: Fire
Chakra: Root & Heart
Color: #F87171
ColorVibration: Crimson / Scarlet
EsotericSignature: Life force, compassion, the original heat of the heart. Mars provides the passionate drive; the Sun provides the spiritual fire of Anahata.
BiologicalSystem: Cardiovascular & Adrenal
BiologicalMechanism: Neferine and nuciferine alkaloids relax arterial smooth muscle and reduce platelet aggregation. Red proanthocyanidins are powerfully anti-inflammatory and support healthy blood pressure.
EndocrineTarget: Adrenal glands, thymus (heart-immune axis)
NervousSystem: Activates the cardiac plexus; supports vagal tone through cardiovascular regulation; reduces adrenal cortisol output
WellnessBenefits: Heart health and circulatory vitality, Compassion and emotional courage activation, Adrenal support and vitality restoration, Anti-inflammatory cardiovascular tonic
TeaSafe: true
TeaProfile: Mild, lightly floral, delicate. Steep dried petals 5-7 min at 190°F.
TraditionalUse: Offered in Buddhist and Hindu practices for compassion, love, and the bodhisattva heart. Sacred to Avalokiteshvara.
BodyPlacement: Heart center (sternum), base of spine (root activation)
SafetyNote: Generally safe. Mild blood-pressure-lowering effect — use with caution if on antihypertensive medication.
KitEligible: true

Variety: White Lotus

SecondaryPlanet: Venus

BiologicalSystem: Neurological & Reproductive (Feminine)
BiologicalMechanism: Isoliensinine and nuciferoline are sedative alkaloids that modulate serotonin receptors and promote restorative sleep. Supports HPA axis downregulation under chronic stress and upregulates BDNF in the hippocampus.
EndocrineTarget: Pituitary gland, pineal gland (melatonin), ovaries
NervousSystem: Calms CNS; promotes parasympathetic dominance; supports melatonin production and circadian rhythm regulation
WellnessBenefits: Deep restorative sleep and lucid dreaming, Emotional clarity and subconscious processing, Hormonal balance (feminine cycle regulation), Neuroprotection and stress recovery
TeaSafe: true
TeaProfile: Sweet, delicate, slightly honeyed. Dried petals or stamens. Steep 5 min at 185°F.
TraditionalUse: Offered to Saraswati (wisdom) and Quan Yin (compassion). Used in lunar practices and for divine feminine invocation.
BodyPlacement: Crown of head, lower abdomen (uterus/sacral for cycle support)
SafetyNote: Safe. Mild sedative — do not combine with prescription sleep medications or sedatives.
KitEligible: true

BiologicalSystem: Neuro-Endocrine Axis (Dopaminergic)

EndocrineTarget: Pituitary gland, hypothalamus, dopamine system
NervousSystem: Dopaminergic regulation; reduces hyperactive neural firing; supports clear perception and sustained attention
WellnessBenefits: Elevates baseline dopamine — lifts mood and motivation, Promotes lucid, deeply restorative sleep, Supports sustained mental clarity and perception, Neuroprotective — reduces cortisol-driven brain inflammation
TeaSafe: true

TraditionalUse: Buddhist iconography: the blue lotus signifies complete enlightenment. Used in Tibetan thangka paintings to represent Manjushri (wisdom deity).
BodyPlacement: Third eye (between brows), throat
SafetyNote: Safe in tea form. Contains mild …

NervousSystem: Activates limbic-vagal circuit; promotes theta brainwave states (4-8 Hz) associated with deep meditation, dreaming, and spiritual vision
WellnessBenefits: Deep meditation and lucid dreaming support, Anxiety and emotional tension relief, Spiritual perception and intuitive opening, Gentle mood elevation without stimulant effect
TeaSafe: true
TeaProfile: Delicate, slightly sweet, mildly euphoric warmth. Steep 10-15 min at 180°F. Ancient recipe: steeped in grape juice or wine. Tea is the modern equivalent.
TraditionalUse: Most sacred flower of Ancient Egypt. Associated with Ra, Osiris, and the cr…

chunks.text:
Description: How the Astryx engine should use this file alongside the other data files to produce the unified 5-sense prescription.
Step1 InputCollection: User reports: symptom OR feeling OR area of concern. Engine receives natal chart + current transits.
Step2 DiagnosticMatch: Engine looks up symptom in rootCauseIndex → returns candidate planetary signatures. Cross-references with user's natal chart and current transits → ranks signatures by activation.
Step3 AnatomyResolution: For top-ranked planetary signature, engine pulls anatomy block → body systems + organs + endocrine + nervous system pathway.
Step4 PlainLanguageBridge: Engine pulls plainLanguageBridge → presents 3 layers: what's happening cosmically, what it's doing in your body, what to do.
Step5 ProtocolGeneration: Engine pulls crossReferences from this planet → links to: cellSalts.json (mineral via ruling sign), sacredBotanicals.json (plant ally), crystalsExpanded.json (mineral resonance), sacredTones_nervousSystem.json (frequency), scents.json (aroma), colors.json (visual), planets.json (taste/body movement). One unified prescription, five sensory channels.
Step6 PractitionerOverlay: In Practitioner mode, all three layers (cosmic + body + protocol) are visible with timing data (transit duration, intervention notes). In free user mode, only middle (body) and bottom (protocol) layers shown — cosmic mechanism is the optional 'See Why' reveal.
chunks.source: Synthesizes Hippocratic medical astrology, Nicholas Culpeper (1652), Cornell's Encyclopaedia of Medical Astrology (1933), Charles Carter, Donna Cunningham, Eileen Nauman, and modern psychosomatic medicine. Cross-validated with Carey's biochemic system.

Conjunction: Fused and immediate —
Opposition: Stretched across a full axis, asking for balance —
Square: Under friction that wants movement —
Trine: In easy, open flow —
Sextile: A door standing open —
Quincunx: At an odd angle, asking for adjustment —

Tier: 2

EndocrineTarget: Thymus gland (immune-endocrine bridge), adrenal cortex
NervousSystem: Cardiac plexus activation; supports parasympathetic heart regulation via vagal tone
BrainwaveAffinity: Alpha (8-12 Hz) — heart coherence, radiant presence
WellnessBenefits: Cardiovascular vitality, Immune system fortification, Heart coherence and emotional courage, Anti-inflammatory support
TeaSafe: true

TraditionalUse: Solar practices, heart-opening practices, offerings for love and courage
BodyPlacement: Heart center (sternum), solar plexus
SafetyNote: Safe. Mild hypotensive — caution with blood pressure medications.
KitProduct: Red Lotus Petals (dried)
KitEligible: true

KitId: planetary-sign-kit
KitName: Planetary Calibration Kit
Tagline: Your sign. Your medicine. Your stones.
Description: One kit per sun sign — each contains the ruling planet's Sacred Botanical tea, featured crystal (polished, tumbled), a printed mini Blueprint card with your sign's Hz frequency, plant ally, and body placement guide, and a sealed keepsake pouch.
Variants: 12 variants — one per sun sign (Aries through Pisces)
Contents: Sacred Botanical herb (ruling planet) — 1 oz dried, sealed, Featured crystal — 1 polished tumbled stone, Mini Blueprint Card — printed, sign-specific: Hz frequency, plant ally, crystal, body placement, Organza or linen keepsake pouch, QR code linking to the user's Astryx blueprint
ShopLink: https://sacredtea.net
ProposedPrice: $28-38 USD
AppIntegration: When user views their Cosmic Blueprint, a 'Shop Your Kit' card appears below Plant Allies with their sign-specific kit. One-click to sacredtea.net product page.
KitEligible: true

KitId: lotus-spectrum-collection
KitName: The Lotus Spectrum Collection
Tagline: Four lotus. Five planets. One sacred lineage.
Description: The complete Lotus Spectrum — all four lotus varieties (Red, White, Blue, Egyptian Blue) as individual dried flower packets, plus four corresponding crystals (one per lotus), a Lotus Planetary Matrix card, and a four-cup brewing & intention guide.
Contents: Red Lotus petals — 0.5 oz dried (Mars/Sun), White Lotus petals/stamens — 0.5 oz dried (Moon/Venus), Blue Lotus flowers — 0.5 oz dried (Jupiter), Egyptian Blue Lotus flowers — 0.5 oz dried (Neptune), Four crystal set: Red Jasper, Moonstone, Lapis Lazuli, Labradorite (all tumbled), Lotus Planetary Matrix card — full color, laminated, Brewing & Intention Guide — brewing temps, intentions, safety notes, Gift box packaging
ShopLink: https://sacredtea.net
ProposedPrice: $58-72 USD
AppIntegration: Featured in the Plant Allies tab as 'The Lotus Collection — cross-planetary medicine.' Visible to all users regardless of sign. Prominent in the Welcome Screen shop section.
KitEligible: true

KitId: practitioner-calibration-bundle
KitName: Practitioner Calibration Bundle
Tagline: The full system. For the professional practitioner.
Description: The complete physical toolkit for Astryx-certified practitioners — 7 planetary Sacred Botanical teas, 7 planetary crystals, a full-color Practitioner Body Layout Map, and a Sacred Tones Session Guide card listing all 13 planetary Hz frequencies and bone application points.
Contents: 7 Sacred Botanical teas — one per traditional planet (Sun through Saturn): Red Lotus, White Lotus, Lavender, Rose, Red Hibiscus, Blue Lotus, Horsetail (0.5 oz each), 7 featured crystals — one per traditional planet: Amber, Moonstone, Blue Lace Agate, Rose Quartz, Bloodstone, Lapis Lazuli, Shungite (all polished, tumbled), Practitioner Body Layout Map — full-color, laminated, 8.5x11 — shows crystal placement points over body outline, Sacred Tones Session Guide card — all 13 planetary Hz, bone application points, vagal strength indicators, Velvet or linen storage pouch for crystals, Wooden or bamboo herb storage box, QR code to Astryx Practitioner Portal
ShopLink: https://sacredtea.net
ProposedPrice: $125-158 USD
AppIntegration: Promoted in Practitioner Portal dashboard as 'Complete your practice with the physical toolkit.' Shown in Session Mode alongside Sacred Tones fork map. Link in PDF Blueprint Report footer.
KitEligible: true

KitId: sacred-botanicals-tea-collection
KitName: Sacred Botanicals Tea Collection
Tagline: Nine master plants. Nine planetary medicines.
Description: All nine Sacred Botanicals as individual tea samples — the complete cross-planetary botanical library in one collection. Includes brewing guide and planetary correspondence card.
Contents: Red Lotus (Sun/Mars) — 0.5 oz, White Lotus (Moon/Venus) — 0.5 oz, Lavender (Mercury) — 0.5 oz culinary grade, Rose petals (Venus) — 0.5 oz organic, Red Hibiscus (Mars) — 0.5 oz, Blue Lotus (Jupiter) — 0.5 oz, Horsetail (Saturn) — 0.5 oz, Egyptian Blue Lotus (Neptune) — 0.5 oz, Damiana (Pluto) — 0.5 oz, Sacred Botanicals Brewing Guide — temperatures, timing, intentions, safety, Planetary Correspondence Card
ShopLink: https://sacredtea.net
ProposedPrice: $45-62 USD
AppIntegration: Accessible from all blueprint views as 'Explore the full Sacred Botanicals collection.' Primary upsell for individual users who want to go deeper.
KitEligible: true

chunks.text:
Name: Cardio-Circulatory Axis
Polarity: Leo ↔ Aquarius
Source: Minerva (2023), Axa cardio-circulatorie
Interpretation: Leo governs the heart (the central pump); Aquarius governs peripheral circulation and oxygen exchange in tissues. The axis represents heart-pump versus tissue-perfusion. Imbalance toward Leo may manifest as central cardiac overactivity (hypertension, palpitation, angina). Imbalance toward Aquarius may manifest as peripheral circulation issues, cold extremities, tissue oxygen deficit.
IndicatorPattern: Leo Dominant: Heart palpitation, pounding pulse, hypertensive flush, central cardiac symptoms, chest tightness · Aquarius Dominant: Cold extremities, varicose veins, peripheral cyanosis, tissue hypoxia signs, ankle swelling · Balanced: Steady pulse, even peripheral perfusion, smooth cardiac rhythm

chunks.text:
Name: Emotional / Structural Axis (cardiac overlay)
Polarity: Cancer ↔ Capricorn
Source: Minerva (2023), Axa emoţională şi structurală
Interpretation: Though primarily emotional/structural, this axis affects the cardiovascular system via the heart-mind connection. Cancer (Moon-ruled) governs the emotional component of cardiac response — palpitation under emotional stress. Capricorn (Saturn-ruled) governs structural integrity of vascular walls — atherosclerosis, vascular calcification. Together they describe the hypertension-from-suppressed-emotion pattern.

chunks.text:
Name: Renal Axis (cardio-renal overlay)
Polarity: Aries ↔ Libra
Source: Minerva (2023), Axa renală
Interpretation: The kidneys regulate blood pressure via renin-angiotensin-aldosterone. Aries (Mars/adrenal) and Libra (Venus/kidney) form the polarity that affects cardiovascular pressure. Hypertension often signals dysregulation on this axis as much as on the cardio-circulatory axis.

chunks.text:
ScopeOfPractice: Cardiovascular conditions can be life-threatening. Output to Individual tier emphasizes monitoring and referral. Practitioner tier receives full clinical signatures with explicit deferral to MD for diagnosis/treatment. Verified Practitioner tier additionally receives detailed contraindication panels for bodywork.
IndicatorLanguagePolicy: All output respects COMPLIANCE.md §3 — uses approved phrasings only. Specific pathology names rendered to Practitioner+ tiers only; Individual tier sees generic descriptors ('circulatory patterns', 'vascular tendencies').
CriticalReferralRules: ANY acute chest pain → 911 (do not pass through normal app flow), Sudden severe headache + cardiovascular signs → 911, BP > 180/120 with symptoms → emergency, Suspected DVT, dissection, or rupture → urgent MD, Bodywork on known cardiac history requires medical clearance
RedFlagKeywords: chest pain, crushing, tearing, radiating arm, diaphoresis, fainting, tachycardia at rest

chunks.text:
PrimaryStructures: Mouth, salivary glands, tongue, teeth, Esophagus (~25 cm tube), Stomach (cardia, fundus, body, pylorus), Small intestine (duodenum, jejunum, ileum) — ~6m total, Large intestine (cecum, ascending/transverse/descending/sigmoid colon), Rectum and anus, Liver (largest gland in body) + biliary tree (gallbladder, bile ducts), Pancreas (exocrine acini + endocrine islets), Mesenteric vasculature + enteric nervous system ("second brain")
SubStructures: Upper Gi: Mouth, Salivary glands (parotid, submandibular, sublingual), Esophagus, Stomach · Small Intestine: Duodenum (digestion, bile/pancreatic juice entry), Jejunum (primary absorption), Ileum (B12 absorption, ileocecal valve) · Large Intestine: Cecum + appendix, Ascending colon, Transverse colon, Descending colon, Sigmoid colon, Rectum, Anal canal · Accessory Organs: Liver, Gallbladder, Bile ducts, Pancreas (head/body/tail) · Enteric Nervous System: Myenteric plexus (Auerbach's — motility), Submucosal plexus (Meissner's — secretion/blood flow), ~500 million neurons
VertebralCorrelates: Per Cornell: 5th-9th dorsal vertebrae relate to upper GI (stomach, esophagus); 10th-12th dorsal relate to lower GI, small intestine, liver; lumbar L1-L5 relates to colon, bladder, kidneys. Bodyworkers: thoracolumbar mobilization + visceral manipulation directly supports digestive function.
NervePlexus: Celiac plexus (solar plexus — sympathetic; major digestive innervation), Superior mesenteric plexus, Inferior mesenteric plexus. Vagus nerve (parasympathetic — rest-and-digest). Enteric nervous system semi-autonomous.

chunks.text:
Name: Digestive-Immune Axis
Polarity: Virgo ↔ Pisces
Source: Minerva (2023), Axa digestivă-imunitară
Interpretation: Virgo governs digestion and assimilation; Pisces governs immune function and dissolution. Modern medicine confirms: ~70% of immune tissue lives in the gut (GALT). Imbalance toward Virgo may manifest as analytical/picky digestion, IBS-anxiety patterns, hyperdigestion. Imbalance toward Pisces may manifest as food sensitivities, leaky gut, immune-mediated GI symptoms. The axis is THE foundation of modern functional medicine's gut-immune model.
IndicatorPattern: Virgo Dominant: Picky digestion, IBS-type symptoms, anxiety-gut axis, perfectionistic eating patterns, hyperacid burn · Pisces Dominant: Food sensitivities, leaky gut, immune-mediated GI, vague chronic digestive symptoms with no clear cause · Balanced: Smooth digestion, varied diet tolerated, robust immune-gut interface

chunks.text:
Name: Regenerative Axis (digestive overlay — colon/elimination)
Polarity: Taurus ↔ Scorpio
Source: Minerva (2023), Axa regenerării
Interpretation: Taurus (throat, upper digestive intake) opposite Scorpio (colon, elimination, transformation). The axis describes the complete digestive tube from mouth to anus. Chronic constipation = Scorpio side underactive; over-elimination/diarrhea = Scorpio overactive.

PrimaryStructures: Pineal gland (epithalamus — melatonin, possibly DMT), Pituitary (anterior + posterior — master gland, controls others), Hypothalamus (links nervous + endocrine — releasing hormones), Thyroid (T3/T4 — metabolic rate), Parathyroid (calcium regulation), Thymus (immune-endocrine bridge — declines with age), Adrenals (cortex: cortisol, aldosterone, DHEA; medulla: epinephrine, norepinephrine), Pancreas (alpha: glucagon; beta: insulin; delta: somatostatin), Gonads (ovaries: estrogen, progesterone; testes: testosterone)

NervePlexus: Cardiac plexus (thymus), celiac plexus (adrenals, pancreas), pelvic plexus (gonads). Vagal influence on most endocrine functions.

Symptoms vary widely — individualize protocols. Bioidentical hormone replacement (BHRT) is appropriate for many; coordinate with prescribing MD (gynecologist or NAMS-certified menopause specialist). Black cohosh, sage (hot flashes), maca, vitex evidence-supported herbals. Bone density baseline (DEXA) post-menopause critical.

RedFlags: Psoriatic arthritis joint symptoms → rheumatology

chunks.text:
ScopeOfPractice: Skin lesions require visual inspection. ALWAYS refer suspicious lesions (asymmetry, irregular borders, multiple colors, large diameter, evolving — ABCDE) for dermatology evaluation. Holistic adjuncts complement.
CriticalReferralRules: Suspicious mole (ABCDE positive) → dermatology, Stevens-Johnson Syndrome (skin sloughing) → ED, Erythroderma (>90% body redness) → urgent dermatology, Cellulitis (red hot tender spreading) → urgent MD, Severe burns → ED
RedFlagKeywords: spreading redness, skin peeling sheets, mole changing, severe burn

system.name: Lymphatic & Immune System
system.shortDescription: Lymphatic drainage network + immune defense. Lymph nodes, vessels, spleen, thymus, tonsils, MALT/GALT. Innate + adaptive immunity. The body's surveillance and clearance system.

chunks.text:
PrimaryStructures: Lymphatic vessels (parallel network to vascular), Lymph nodes (~600 throughout body — clusters: cervical, axillary, inguinal, mesenteric), Spleen (largest secondary lymphoid organ), Thymus (T-cell maturation — atrophies after puberty), Tonsils + adenoids (oropharyngeal immune), Mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue (MALT) — GALT (gut), BALT (bronchi), Bone marrow (B-cell maturation, all blood cells), White blood cells: neutrophils, lymphocytes (T, B, NK), monocytes/macrophages, eosinophils, basophils
SubStructures: Primary Lymphoid: Bone marrow, Thymus · Secondary Lymphoid: Spleen, Lymph nodes, Tonsils, MALT/GALT · Lymph Flow: Capillaries → collecting vessels → trunks → ducts (right lymphatic + thoracic) → venous return · Innate Immunity: Physical barriers, Neutrophils, Macrophages, NK cells, Complement · Adaptive Immunity: T cells (cellular), B cells / antibodies (humoral), Memory cells
VertebralCorrelates: Thymus: T2-T4. Spleen: T6-T9 (left side). Major lymph clusters supplied by adjacent spinal nerves.
NervePlexus: Sympathetic innervation of spleen, thymus, lymph nodes. Vagal influence on GALT and immune-gut signaling.

IndicatorLanguage: Individual: Post-viral chronic fatigue (including long COVID) is real and increasingly recognized. Pacing (do less than you think you can), avoiding post-exertional malaise, mitochondrial support, addressing co-infections, sleep optimization — gradual recovery is possible for many. · Practitioner: Neptune-Sun configurations align with vitality-dissolution patterns. ME-CFS criteria specific (post-exertional malaise central). Pacing primary intervention. Mitochondrial cofactors (CoQ10, ribose, magnesium). Reactivated viral testing (EBV, HHV-6). Refer to ME-CFS-knowledgeable physicians.
ModalityNotes: Bodywork: VERY gentle — clients have post-exertional malaise. Short sessions. · Lifestyle: Pacing, energy envelope, sleep, gentle movement (within tolerance), nutritional support
RedFlags: New cardiac symptoms (POTS often comorbid) → cardiology

Cooling intention. · Lifestyle: RICE first 48-72hr. Arnica gel topical. Bromelain (pineapple enzyme) for swelling. Ginger anti-inflammatory.

SubStructures: Central: Cerebral cortex (frontal, parietal, temporal, occipital), Limbic system (amygdala, hippocampus, hypothalamus), Basal ganglia, Thalamus, Cerebellum, Brainstem (midbrain, pons, medulla), Spinal cord · Peripheral Motor: Anterior horn cells → spinal motor nerves → muscles · Peripheral Sensory: Dorsal root ganglia → posterior horn → ascending tracts → thalamus → cortex · Autonomic Sympathetic: Thoracolumbar outflow, Paravertebral chain, Adrenal medulla connection · Autonomic Parasympathetic: Cranial + sacral outflow, Vagus nerve (CN X) — primary parasympathetic · Enteric: Myenteric plexus, Submucosal plexus, ~500 million neurons in gut
VertebralCorrelates: ENTIRE SPINE. Per Cornell: each vertebral level corresponds to specific nerve roots and downstream organ systems. C1-C7 → head/neck/upper limb + cardiac/respiratory parasympathetic (vagus). T1-T12 → thoracic organs + sympathetic chain. L1-L5 → lower limb + pelvic. S1-S5 → pelvic parasympathetic. Bodyworkers: spinal mobilization is nervous system intervention.

Behavioral interventions (CBT-I) most evidence-based; herbal/supplement adjuncts complement.

chunks.text:
ScopeOfPractice: Nervous system pathology spans acute emergencies (stroke, seizure, status migrainosus) to chronic conditions (anxiety, depression, neuropathy). Crisis keyword detection is CRITICAL for this system. Mental health particularly requires referral to licensed mental health professionals — holistic practice is adjunctive only.
IndicatorLanguagePolicy: Per COMPLIANCE.md §3. Mental health specifically: avoid pathologizing language for Individual tier — frame as 'patterns' rather than diagnosed conditions.
CriticalReferralRules: Suicidal ideation → 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline immediately, Sudden severe headache (worst of life) → 911 (rule out hemorrhage), Focal neurological deficit (sudden weakness, numbness, vision/speech changes) → 911 (rule out stroke — FAST protocol), Active seizure → 911 if first-time, > 5 min, or recovery delayed, Severe acute mental health crisis (psychosis, mania, danger to self/others) → mental health emergency, Severe depression with hopelessness → mental health referral within 24-48 hours
RedFlagKeywords: suicide, want to die, kill myself, worst headache, can't move, vision loss, seizure, hallucinations, hearing voices

Reproductive endocrinology offers many options. · Practitioner: Saturn in 5th or to reproductive significators classically associated with fertility challenges (Cornell, 1933). Both partners need workup. Refer to reproductive endocrinology if appropriate. Holistic adjuncts: comprehensive nutrition (CoQ10, vitamin D, methylated folate, omega-3), stress management, acupuncture (evidence-supported), maca, shatavari (women), tribulus (men).

Refer to NAMS-certified menopause specialist for comprehensive care.
ModalityNotes: Bodywork: Hormone-related joint/muscle changes — adjust pressure · Lifestyle: Strength training, calcium/D/K2, sleep, stress, social connection, BHRT consideration
RedFlags: Postmenopausal bleeding → urgent gyn (always abnormal)

system.name: Respiratory System
system.shortDescription: Lungs, airways, and respiratory muscles — gas exchange (O₂ in, CO₂ out) and breath as the body's primary autonomic interface

chunks.text:
PrimaryStructures: Nasal cavity, sinuses, pharynx (upper airway), Larynx (voice box) and trachea (windpipe), Bronchi (primary, secondary, tertiary) and bronchioles, Lungs — right (3 lobes) and left (2 lobes), Alveoli (gas exchange units, ~300 million per adult), Pleura (parietal + visceral) and pleural fluid, Diaphragm (primary breathing muscle), Accessory muscles: intercostals, scalenes, SCM, abdominals (forced breathing)
SubStructures: Upper Airway: Nasal cavity, Paranasal sinuses, Pharynx, Larynx · Lower Airway: Trachea, Right + left main bronchus, Lobar bronchi, Segmental bronchi, Bronchioles, Alveolar ducts, Alveoli · Gas Exchange: Alveolar epithelium, Pulmonary capillary, Diffusion barrier (~0.5 μm) · Breathing Muscles: Diaphragm, External + internal intercostals, Scalenes (accessory), SCM (accessory), Abdominal wall (forced exhale)
VertebralCorrelates: Per Cornell: 3rd-5th dorsal vertebrae relate to lungs and bronchi. T1-T5 thoracic mobility critical for full breath expansion. Cervical spine C3-C5 supplies phrenic nerve (diaphragm innervation). Bodyworkers: T1-T8 mobilization + cervical decompression directly supports breathing capacity.
NervePlexus: Pulmonary plexus (sympathetic + parasympathetic via vagus nerve). Phrenic nerve (C3-C5) controls diaphragm. Vagal regulation = parasympathetic bronchoconstriction baseline.

chunks.text:
Name: Respiratory Axis
Polarity: Gemini ↔ Sagittarius
Source: Minerva (2023), Axa respiratorie
Interpretation: Gemini governs the upper lungs and bronchi (inhalation, intake, signal); Sagittarius governs the diaphragm and lower lung capacity (exhalation, expansion, broad breath). The axis represents breath as both intake AND release. Imbalance toward Gemini may manifest as shallow rapid breathing, upper-chest tension, anxiety-pattern breath. Imbalance toward Sagittarius may manifest as diaphragmatic restriction, full-chest collapse, breath that won't deepen.
IndicatorPattern: Gemini Dominant: Shallow upper-chest breathing, rapid rate, anxiety-pattern hyperventilation, bronchial irritability · Sagittarius Dominant: Diaphragmatic restriction, sighing repeatedly, breath that won't deepen, mid-back/diaphragm tension · Balanced: Smooth full diaphragmatic breath cycle, balanced inhalation/exhalation rhythm

ScopeOfPractice: Respiratory conditions range from minor (mild cold) to life-threatening (pneumonia, severe asthma attack, pulmonary embolism). Holistic practice is adjunctive to medical care. Individual tier emphasizes monitoring + referral; Practitioner tier provides clinical signatures with explicit deferral to MD for confirmed pathology.
IndicatorLanguagePolicy: All output respects COMPLIANCE.md §3. Specific pathology names rendered to Practitioner+ tiers only; Individual tier sees 'respiratory patterns', 'breath tendencies'.
CriticalReferralRules: Acute severe dyspnea → 911, Hemoptysis → urgent MD, Asthma attack with peak flow < 50% → 911 or ED, Suspected pulmonary embolism (sudden dyspnea + chest pain + leg swelling) → 911, O₂ saturation < 92% → urgent medical evaluation, Cyanosis (blue lips/fingertips) → 911
RedFlagKeywords: can't breathe, blue lips, coughing blood, chest pain breathing, severe shortness of breath

chunks.text:
PrimaryStructures: Skull (cranial + facial bones, 22 total), Spine (33 vertebrae — 7 cervical, 12 thoracic, 5 lumbar, 5 fused sacral, 4 fused coccygeal), Thorax (ribs, sternum), Upper extremity (clavicle, scapula, humerus, radius, ulna, carpals, metacarpals, phalanges), Pelvis (ilium, ischium, pubis), Lower extremity (femur, patella, tibia, fibula, tarsals, metatarsals, phalanges), Joints (synovial, cartilaginous, fibrous), Cartilage (hyaline, fibrocartilage, elastic), Ligaments (bone-to-bone), Bone marrow (red — hematopoiesis; yellow — fat storage), Tooth enamel + dentin
SubStructures: Axial: Skull, Vertebral column, Ribs, Sternum · Appendicular: Pectoral girdle, Upper limb, Pelvic girdle, Lower limb · Joint Types: Synovial (most movable — knees, hips, shoulders), Cartilaginous (intervertebral discs, pubic symphysis), Fibrous (skull sutures) · Weight Bearing: Spine, Pelvis, Femur, Tibia, Calcaneus
VertebralCorrelates: ENTIRE SPINAL COLUMN is part of this system. Per Cornell + osteopathic tradition: each vertebral level affects downstream organs through its emerging nerve roots. C1 (atlas) - sometimes considered most influential single bone.
NervePlexus: All major plexus pass through skeletal structure. Vertebral foramina protect nerve roots.

Id: skel_osteoporosis
Name: Osteoporosis / Osteopenia
IcdReference: M80 / M81
System: skeletal
Category: bone_density
AstrologicalSignature: PlanetaryCombinations: Saturn-Moon (chronic mineral loss), Saturn-Venus (post-estrogen decline), Saturn in 6th house · TransitTriggers: Saturn return (especially 2nd — age 58-60), Pluto-Moon (deep hormonal transformation)
ClassicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Atrophy of bone — Saturn afflictions over time; deficiency of bone-building cell salts.' Carey: 'Calc Phos deficiency = bone weakness.'
CellSalts: Calc Phos (PRIMARY), Calc Fluor, Silica
IndicatorLanguage: Individual: Bone health benefits from weight-bearing exercise (most important), adequate calcium + D + K2 + magnesium, sufficient protein, and addressing risk factors (smoking, excess alcohol, low body weight). DEXA scan after age 50 (women) or 70 (men) gives baseline; earlier if risk factors. · Practitioner: Saturn-Moon and Saturn-Venus configurations classically align with bone density loss patterns. Modern: DEXA T-score, FRAX risk assessment. Calcium, D3, K2, magnesium, strontium, boron supportive. Refer for endocrinology/rheumatology — bisphosphonates, denosumab, parathyroid hormone analogs may be appropriate.
ModalityNotes: Bodywork: NO HVLA manipulation. Gentle mobilization only. Postural work to reduce fall risk. · Reiki: Bone-direct intention. Root chakra activation. · Lifestyle: Weight-bearing + resistance exercise (most evidence-based). Adequate protein. Comprehensive bone nutrient stack. Fall prevention. Address contributing factors.
RedFlags: Suspected vertebral comp…

Id: skel_osteoarthritis
Name: Osteoarthritis
IcdReference: M15-M19
System: skeletal
Category: degenerative_joint
AstrologicalSignature: PlanetaryCombinations: Saturn afflicted in cardinal signs (joints), Saturn-Mars (long-term wear with inflammatory component) · TransitTriggers: Saturn transit
ClassicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Saturn in joints → restriction, hardening, degenerative change over time.'
CellSalts: Calc Fluor, Silica, Nat Phos (acid component)
IndicatorLanguage: Individual: Joint pain that worsens with use and improves with rest, morning stiffness < 30 min — gentle movement is medicine. Strengthening surrounding muscles, maintaining healthy weight, addressing biomechanics, anti-inflammatory diet, glucosamine/chondroitin (modest evidence), turmeric all supportive. Severe pain or joint deformity → orthopedics. · Practitioner: Saturn-cardinal sign configurations classically associated with degenerative joint patterns. Differentiate OA (mechanical) from RA (autoimmune — different signature). Boswellia, turmeric/curcumin, omega-3, glucosamine sulfate, MSM all evidence-supported adjuncts. Refer for orthopedics for severe joint destruction (joint replacement consideration).
ModalityNotes: Bodywork: Gentle joint mobilization, surrounding muscle release, fascial work. Avoid aggressive on inflamed joint. · Reiki: Joint-specific healing. · Lifestyle: Movement (not rest — joints need motion to stay healthy), weight management, anti-inflammatory diet, supplements above.
RedFlags: Hot red swollen joint with fever → rule out septic arthritis (ED), Sudden severe pain dispr…

Healing support: protein, calcium, magnesium, vitamin D, vitamin C (collagen), vitamin K2, comfrey topical (root, not internal). · Practitioner: Mars-Saturn transits classically associated with fracture timing. Acute: refer for imaging/orthopedics. Healing phase: support with nutrient stack above. Comfrey (Symphytum, classical bone-knit) topical for closed fractures. AVOID stimulating modalities over acute fracture.
ModalityNotes: Bodywork: AVOID over acute fracture. Recovery phase (post-immobilization): gentle scar tissue work, joint mobilization, surrounding muscle work for strength rebuilding. · Reiki: Excellent for healing support — non-touching distance reiki ideal for casted limbs. · Lifestyle: Comprehensive bone nutrient stack. Protein adequacy. Avoid NSAIDs (impair bone healing — evidence). Movement of unaffected areas to maintain conditioning.
RedFlags: Open fracture (bone through skin) → 911, Spine fracture…

IcdReference: M05-M06
System: skeletal
Category: autoimmune_inflammatory
AstrologicalSignature: PlanetaryCombinations: Mars-Saturn-Neptune (autoimmune joint pattern), Pluto-Saturn (deep transformation of structure) · TransitTriggers: Outer planet transits to natal Saturn

CellSalts: Nat Phos, Calc Fluor, Ferr Phos (flares)
IndicatorLanguage: Individual: Symmetric joint pain (especially small joints of hands/feet) with morning stiffness > 30 min and fatigue requires rheumatology evaluation. RA is a serious autoimmune condition requiring medical management. · Practitioner: Mars-Saturn-Neptune complexes classically associated with autoimmune inflammatory joint patterns. RA is rheumatology territory — DMARDs, biologics. Holistic adjuncts: anti-inflammatory diet (Mediterranean, autoimmune protocol), omega-3, turmeric, vitamin D, gut health (gut-joint axis), stress management. NEVER replace rheumatology care.
ModalityNotes: Bodywork: Active flare: gentle only. Remission: more active work appropriate. Always within client tolerance. · Reiki: Supportive throughout. Joint-specific + immune intention. · Lifestyle: AIP (autoimmune protocol) diet trial. Anti-inflammatory foods. Address gut health. Stress management. Movement within tolerance.
RedFlags: Cervical spine RA involvement → urgent (atlantoaxial instability), Vasculitis features (skin, eyes, lungs) → urgent rheumatology

IcdReference: M51
System: skeletal
Category: discogenic
AstrologicalSignature: PlanetaryCombinations: Saturn-Mars in Libra/Scorpio, Uranus-Mars (sudden disc rupture) · TransitTriggers: Uranus transit, Saturn-Mars combinations

IndicatorLanguage: Individual: Acute disc-pattern pain (often with sciatic radiation down the leg) typically improves with: avoiding aggravating positions, gentle movement (walking), nerve glides, and time. Severe weakness, bowel/bladder symptoms, or saddle anesthesia require immediate medical evaluation. · Practitioner: Saturn-Mars in Libra/Scorpio classically associated with lumbar disc patterns. Most disc herniations resolve with conservative care (movement, PT, time). Imaging (MRI) only changes management for surgical candidates or red flags. Refer for ortho/neuro if red flags or refractory > 6 weeks.
ModalityNotes: Bodywork: AVOID flexion-rotation forces. Gentle traction, nerve glides, surrounding musculature. McKenzie extension approach for many disc patterns. · Reiki: Sacral + root chakras. · Lifestyle: Movement (not bed rest). McGill big 3 (curl-up, side plank, bird dog) for spine stability. Hip mobility. Address ergonomics.
RedFlags: Bowel/bladder dysfunction → cauda equina, ED, Saddle anesthesia → ED, Progressive motor weakness → urgent neurosurgical

chunks.text:
PrimaryStructures: Kidneys (paired, retroperitoneal — T12-L3 level), Renal cortex + medulla + pyramids, Nephrons (~1 million per kidney) — glomerulus, Bowman's capsule, proximal tubule, loop of Henle, distal tubule, collecting duct, Renal pelvis, Ureters (~25-30cm each — peristaltic), Bladder (detrusor muscle, trigone), Urethra (longer in male, shorter in female), Sphincters (internal + external), Renal vasculature (renal arteries 20-25% cardiac output)
SubStructures: Filtration Unit: Glomerulus (filtration), Bowman's capsule, PCT (reabsorption), Loop of Henle (concentration), DCT (fine-tuning), Collecting duct (ADH-regulated) · Endocrine Functions: RAAS (renin-angiotensin-aldosterone — BP), Erythropoietin (RBC production), Calcitriol (active vitamin D), Prostaglandins · Voiding Path: Calyces → renal pelvis → ureter → bladder → urethra
VertebralCorrelates: Per Cornell: T11-L2 dorsal-lumbar relates to kidneys, ureters. L1-L2 → bladder. Bodyworkers: lumbar mobilization + psoas (which overlies kidneys) supports urinary function.
NervePlexus: Renal plexus (sympathetic from celiac + aorticorenal). Hypogastric + vesical plexus (bladder). Pudendal nerve (external sphincter).

SupplementaryHerbs: Name: Cranberry · ClassicalAttribution: Venus / Mars · Mechanism: Proanthocyanidins prevent E. coli adhesion to bladder wall · IndicatedFor: UTI prevention only (too late once infection is active); Name: D-Mannose · ClassicalAttribution: Modern · Mechanism: Sugar that E. coli binds to instead of bladder wall · IndicatedFor: UTI prevention + early intervention for E. coli UTI; Name: Uva ursi (bearberry) · ClassicalAttribution: Mars / Saturn · Mechanism: Arbutin → hydroquinone antimicrobial in urine (works in alkaline urine — take with baking soda) · IndicatedFor: Acute UTI — short course only (toxicity if prolonged); Name: Marshmallow root · ClassicalAttribution: Moon / Venus · Mechanism: Demulcent — sooth…

chunks.text:
Id: ren_uti
Name: UTI (Urinary Tract Infection)
IcdReference: N39.0
System: urinary
Category: infection
AstrologicalSignature: PlanetaryCombinations: Mars in Libra/Scorpio (acute inflammation), Mars-Moon (fluid + inflammation), Pluto-Mars in Scorpio · TransitTriggers: Mars transit through Libra/Scorpio
ClassicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Mars in Libra/Scorpio → urinary inflammation, cystitis.'
CellSalts: Ferr Phos, Calc Sulph, Kali Mur
IndicatorLanguage: Individual: UTI symptoms (burning urination, urgency, frequency, suprapubic pain) — early intervention with D-mannose, cranberry, increased fluids may help. If symptoms persist > 24-48 hours, fever develops, back pain occurs, or blood appears in urine — see your healthcare provider for evaluation and likely antibiotics. · Practitioner: Mars-urinary sign configurations classically associated with UTI patterns. Most uncomplicated UTI in healthy adult woman responds to short antibiotic course. Refer for: fever, back pain (rule out pyelonephritis), pregnancy, immunocompromise, men, children, recurrent. Holistic prevention strategies appropriate after acute resolution.
ModalityNotes: Bodywork: Defer during acute. Recovery: pelvic floor work helpful. · Lifestyle: Hydration, D-mannose, cranberry, urinating after intercourse, front-to-back wiping, cotton underwear
RedFlags: Fever + back pain (pyelonephritis) → urgent MD, UTI in pregnancy → MD same day, Blood in urine + fever → urgent

chunks.text:
Id: ren_kidney_stones
Name: Kidney Stones (Nephrolithiasis)
IcdReference: N20
System: urinary
Category: structural
AstrologicalSignature: PlanetaryCombinations: Saturn-Mars in Libra (crystalline obstruction), Saturn in Libra (mineral deposition), Jupiter-Saturn (gout-stone overlap) · TransitTriggers: Saturn transit
ClassicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Renal calculi — Saturn in Libra; mineral deposition under restricted flow.'
CellSalts: Nat Phos (acid type stones), Calc Sulph
IndicatorLanguage: Individual: Kidney stones produce some of the most severe pain in medicine (renal colic). Suspected kidney stone → urgent medical evaluation. Prevention (especially if recurrent): hydration (2-3L/day), reduce oxalate-rich foods if calcium oxalate type, address dietary triggers. · Practitioner: Saturn in Libra classically associated with renal calculi (Cornell, 1933). Acute stones are medical (CT imaging, pain control, possible urology intervention). Prevention: 24-hour urine collection identifies stone type → tailored intervention (low oxalate for Ca-oxalate, low purine for uric acid, etc.). Refer to urology/nephrology.
ModalityNotes: Bodywork: Active colic = ED. Prevention phase: lumbar work, lymphatic drainage. · Lifestyle: Hydration MOST important. Dietary modifications per stone type.
RedFlags: Severe flank pain + nausea/vomiting → ED, Stone with fever → urgent (obstruction + infection)

chunks.text:
Id: ren_incontinence
Name: Urinary Incontinence
IcdReference: N39.3-N39.4
System: urinary
Category: functional
AstrologicalSignature: PlanetaryCombinations: Saturn in Libra (weakened pelvic floor with age), Moon-Saturn (chronic fluid dysregulation) · TransitTriggers: Outer planet transits with age-related onset
ClassicalReference: Cornell (1933): 'Saturn afflictions in urinary signs → loss of control function.'
CellSalts: Calc Fluor (tissue tone), Nat Mur
IndicatorLanguage: Individual: Urinary incontinence is COMMON and TREATABLE — please don't suffer in silence. Pelvic floor physical therapy is highly effective for most types. Specialized care available. · Practitioner: Saturn-urinary configurations align with sphincter weakness patterns. Differentiate: stress (effort/cough/sneeze), urge (overactive bladder), mixed, overflow. Pelvic floor PT first-line. Refer to urogynecology / urology for severe or refractory.
ModalityNotes: Bodywork: Pelvic floor specialized training. Sacral work. AVOID kegels if pelvic floor is HYPERtonic (common mistake — assess first). · Lifestyle: Pelvic floor PT, bladder retraining, weight management, address constipation, avoid irritants (caffeine, alcohol, artificial sweeteners)
RedFlags: Sudden onset with neurological symptoms → urgent (rule out spinal cord)

ScopeOfPractice: Acute renal failure, suspected stones, hematuria, complicated UTI all medical. Holistic adjunctive.
CriticalReferralRules: Severe flank pain + nausea → ED (kidney stone), UTI + fever + back pain → urgent (pyelonephritis), Hematuria (blood in urine) → urgent workup, Anuria (no urine output) → ED, Suspected acute kidney injury → urgent MD, UTI in pregnancy → MD same day
RedFlagKeywords: blood in urine, severe flank pain, no urine, fever back pain
